mnemonics: Rows Row the Boat (Span): The row space is the span of the row vectors, like oarsmen rowing together to move the boat through the vector space.
Columns Carry the Weight (Equations): The column space is the span of the column vectors, which 'carries' all possible outputs b in the equation Ax = b.
keyPoints: The row space of a matrix A is the set of all possible linear combinations of its row vectors. It is a subspace of R^n (where n is the number of columns).
The column space of a matrix A is the set of all possible linear combinations of its column vectors. It is a subspace of R^m (where m is the number of rows).
The dimension of the row space and the dimension of the column space are always equal; this value is called the rank of the matrix.
commonMistakes: Confusing the row space with the column space. Remember: row space is about the rows and lives in R^n; column space is about the columns and lives in R^m.

Assuming the row space and column space are the same subspace. They are generally different as they exist in different dimensional spaces (R^n vs. R^m), unless the matrix is square and has special properties.
